issue.message: Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead

issue.errorLine1: canvas.drawText("Translate: " + String.format("%.2f", vTranslate.x) + ":" + String.format("%.2f", vTranslate.y), 5, 35, debugPaint);
issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead

issue.errorLine1: canvas.drawText("Translate: " + String.format("%.2f", vTranslate.x) + ":" + String.format("%.2f", vTranslate.y), 5, 35, debugPaint);
issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead

issue.errorLine1: canvas.drawText("Source center: " + String.format("%.2f", center.x) + ":" + String.format("%.2f", center.y), 5, 55, debugPaint);
issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead

issue.errorLine1: canvas.drawText("Source center: " + String.format("%.2f", center.x) + ":" + String.format("%.2f", center.y), 5, 55, debugPaint);
issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Reflective access to mEditor, which is not part of the public SDK and therefore likely to change in future Android releases
Reflective access to mIsDropdown, which is not part of the public SDK and therefore likely to change in future Android releases
Reflective access to mAnimationStyle, which is not part of the public SDK and therefore likely to change in future Android releases
Reflective access to mAboveAnchor, which is not part of the public SDK and therefore likely to change in future Android releases
Reflective access to computeAnimationResource, which is not part of the public SDK and therefore likely to change in future Android releases
Avoid using `android.media.ExifInterface`; use `androidx.exifinterface.media.ExifInterface` instead
Not targeting the latest versions of Android; compatibility modes apply. Consider testing and updating this version. Consult the `android.os.Build.VERSION_CODES` javadoc for details.
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future
Accessing internal APIs via reflection is not supported and may not work on all devices or in the future

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Formatting %d followed by words ("images"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("images"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("remaining"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string
Formatting %d followed by words ("more"): This should probably be a plural rather than a string

This folder configuration (`v11`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 24. Merge all the resources in this folder into `drawable`.
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `CloudflareChecker` which has field `webView` pointing to `WebView`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `InterceptingAntiDDOS` which has field `webView` pointing to `WebView`); this is a memory leak
Very long vector path (904 characters), which is bad for performance. Considering reducing precision, removing minor details or rasterizing vector.
Possible overdraw: Root element paints background `?attr/postBackground` with a theme that also paints a background (inferred theme is `@android:style/Theme.Holo`)
